votes.reason: The Python code directly translates the Coq recursive logic and helper functions, preserving the exact behavior for all concrete inputs.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq logic, summing even elements at odd indices using the same recursive approach and helper functions.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq logic, correctly implementing the helper functions and the recursive list traversal to sum even elements at odd indices.

The Python code correctly translates the Coq code. is_even checks if n mod 2 equals 0, is_odd_index checks if the index is odd (using i % 2 != 0 which matches Nat.odd), and add_even_at_odd_indices_aux recursively sums even elements at odd indices starting from index 0. The logic for combining is_odd_index and is_even with 'and' matches the Coq 'andb'. The spec function correctly checks if result equals the computed sum.

The Python code correctly translates the Coq code. is_even checks if n % 2 == 0, is_odd_index checks if i is odd (i % 2 != 0), and add_even_at_odd_indices_aux recursively sums even elements at odd indices starting from index 0. The logic matches the Coq implementation exactly for all concrete inputs.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. is_even checks if n mod 2 equals 0, is_odd_index checks if the index is odd (using i % 2 != 0 which matches Nat.odd), and add_even_at_odd_indices_aux recursively sums even elements at odd indices starting from index 0. The logic matches the Coq implementation exactly for all concrete inputs.
Both sum even elements at odd indices (0-based). Parity checks match Coq’s Z.modulo for negatives, and the recursion/indexing mirrors the Coq definition.
Both compute the sum of elements at odd 0-based indices that are even; parity checks and recursion match Coq’s definitions (including handling negatives via modulo 2).
Both compute the sum of even elements at odd 0-based indices; Python’s % with positive 2 matches Coq’s Z.modulo for negatives, and add_spec checks equality accordingly.
